title: Reject missing request IDs at the input boundary

change.summary: The router now rejects requests without an ID before they can reach the repository.
change.comparison: HEAD -> working tree (staged + unstaged + safe untracked)

files.responsibility: Validate the request ID and pass only valid requests to the repository.
Protect the valid, missing, and not-found request paths.

explanation.goal: Return one predictable client error for malformed requests without doing unnecessary repository work.
observableChanges: A request without an ID now receives a 400 response with request_id_required.
Valid requests keep their existing 200 and 404 behavior.

beforeAfter.before: Every request reached the repository, even when its ID was missing.
beforeAfter.after: The router trims and validates the ID, then stops invalid requests with a 400 response.
beforeAfter.why: Malformed input should be handled at the closest boundary instead of leaking into domain storage.

flow.behavior: Read and normalize the request ID.
Return request_id_required when the normalized ID is empty.
Look up only requests that passed the input guard.

invariants: A valid ID still produces the same 200 or 404 response as before.
A missing ID never causes repository access.

decisions.rationale: The router is the closest boundary that understands the request shape.
decisions.tradeoff: Future entry points must reuse or repeat the same validation rule.

nonGoals: Redesign the full request ID format.
risks: Another entry point could bypass the router-level guard.
Clients may need confirmation that whitespace-only IDs are invalid.

verification.result: This is a representative synthetic demo; no repository command was run.

questions.prompt: What happens after the change when the request ID is missing?

options.text: The repository is queried with an empty ID.
The router returns a 400 error before repository access.
The request always becomes a 404 response.

questions.explanation: The new input guard stops a missing ID at the router and returns request_id_required.

questions.prompt: Which behaviors must remain unchanged for valid IDs?
questions.explanation: The guard changes only malformed requests; valid repository outcomes are invariants.

questions.prompt: What should be checked before adding a second request entry point?

options.text: Whether both entry points share the same ID validation rule.
Whether the repository should be rewritten first.
Whether the existing router tests can be removed.

questions.explanation: Router-level validation is local to one entry point, so a second path could bypass it.

microworld.title: Request path explorer
microworld.instructions: Change the request ID and repository state to compare the before and after paths.

scenarios.title: Valid request with a matching record

steps.behavior: Pass the ID directly to the repository.
Validate the ID, then call the repository.

scenarios.lesson: The successful path keeps its external behavior.

scenarios.title: Valid request without a matching record

steps.behavior: Pass the ID directly to the repository.
Validate the ID, then call the repository.

scenarios.lesson: The valid not-found path also remains stable.

scenarios.title: Missing ID while a record exists

steps.behavior: Pass an empty ID to the repository.
Return an error without repository access.

scenarios.lesson: Malformed input now has one result regardless of repository state.

scenarios.title: Missing ID with no matching record

steps.behavior: Pass an empty ID to the repository.
Perform an unnecessary lookup and return no record.
Return an error without repository access.
